county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1811; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Acadian/Cajun heartland
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. Mary Parish Clerk of Court (Franklin). Formed 1811 from Attakapas County (St. Martin). Sugar plantation country along Bayou Teche. Franklin area antebellum architecture. Chitimacha Tribe of Louisiana reservation at Charenton — oldest continuously occupied community in the Mississippi Valley. Tribal records and museum at Chitimacha Cultural Center. Catholic church registers at Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary (Franklin). Morgan City area — shrimp and oil industry. Extensive enslaved persons records in successions. Cross-reference with St. Martin and Iberia.

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary Parish Conveyance Records — Clerk of Court
source_profiles.repository: St. Mary Parish Clerk of Court, Franklin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Conveyance (deed) books for St. Mary Parish from 1811 forward; Louisiana uses civil law system — property transfers recorded as notarial acts

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary Parish Succession Records — Clerk of Court
source_profiles.repository: St. Mary Parish Clerk of Court, Franklin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Succession records (Louisiana's civil law equivalent of probate) for St. Mary Parish; includes inventories, family meetings (conseil de famille), and partition records naming heirs

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary Parish Marriage Records — Clerk of Court / Catholic Church Registers
source_profiles.repository: St. Mary Parish Clerk of Court / Louisiana State Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil marriage licenses and Catholic church marriage registers for St. Mary Parish; church records often predate civil registration
source_profiles.survival_notes: Statewide vital records registration from 1914; earlier records at parish level and in church registers

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary Parish Catholic Church Sacramental Registers
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of New Orleans / Diocese of Lafayette / Diocese of Baton Rouge / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catholic sacramental registers (baptism, marriage, burial) for St. Mary Parish; includes separate registers for enslaved persons and free people of color in colonial and antebellum periods

source_profiles.source_name: St. Mary Parish Enslaved Persons Records — Clerk of Court / Louisiana State Archives
source_profiles.repository: St. Mary Parish Clerk of Court / Louisiana State Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Enslaved persons records for St. Mary Parish including succession inventories listing enslaved persons by name, age, and value; Catholic church baptismal registers of enslaved persons; Code Noir records; bills of sale

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1810; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Florida Parishes (former Spanish West Florida)
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and St. Tammany Parish Clerk of Court (Covington). Formed 1810 from Spanish West Florida territory. Florida Parishes region — North Shore of Lake Pontchartrain. Covington, Mandeville, and Slidell area. Mixed heritage — Choctaw presence, Anglo-American, French Creole, and German settlers. St. Tammany Parish Library has extensive genealogy collections. Catholic church registers at St. Peter's (Covington). Fontainebleau State Park area (Mandeville). Pre-1810 Spanish colonial records. Southeastern Louisiana University has regional history collections. Cross-reference with Orleans Parish — many New Orleans families had summer homes on the North Shore.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Succession records (Louisiana's civil law equivalent of probate) for St. Tammany Parish; includes inventories, family meetings (conseil de famille), and partition records naming heirs

county_metadata.parent_county: Livingston, St. Helena, St. Tammany, Washington
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1869; Reconstruction-era formation; Florida Parishes (former Spanish West Florida)
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Tangipahoa Parish Clerk of Court (Amite City). Formed 1869 from Livingston, St. Helena, St. Tammany, and Washington. Florida Parishes region. Named for the Tangipahoa River and the Tangipahoa Native Americans. Southeastern Louisiana University (Hammond) has regional collections. Pre-1869 records split among parent parishes. Ponchatoula Strawberry Festival heritage. Italian immigrant community (Independence area — Italian settlement from 1880s). Mixed Anglo-American, Italian, and Creole heritage. Tangipahoa Parish Library has local genealogical collections. Protestant and Catholic church records both important.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1843; antebellum parish; North Louisiana uplands
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Tensas Parish Clerk of Court (St. Joseph). Formed 1843 from Concordia. Mississippi River delta — one of the wealthiest cotton plantation parishes in antebellum Louisiana. Extremely large enslaved population — succession records are critical for enslaved persons research. Tensas River area. St. Joseph area families. Cross-reference with Concordia extensively. Winter Quarters State Historic Site (Grant's Vicksburg Campaign). Tensas Parish Library has local history. Directly across the river from Vicksburg, MS area. Freedmen's Bureau records at NARA document plantation labor transitions.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1822; antebellum parish; Acadian/Cajun heartland
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Terrebonne Parish Clerk of Court (Houma). Formed 1822 from Lafourche. Named for terre bonne (good earth). Houma is named for the Houma Nation — United Houma Nation (state-recognized) has cultural center and community records. Bayou country — sugar plantations and coastal marshes. Catholic church registers at St. Francis de Sales (Houma). Terrebonne Parish Library has genealogy collections. Acadian, French Creole, and Native American heritage. Significant fishing and trapping community records. Cross-reference with Lafourche for Bayou Lafourche families. Oil and gas industry records from 20th century.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1839; antebellum parish; North Louisiana uplands
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Union Parish Clerk of Court (Farmerville). Formed 1839 from Ouachita. North Louisiana uplands — piney woods and cotton farming. Pre-1839 records in Ouachita. Farmerville area families. Anglo-American settlement predominates. Protestant church records (Baptist, Methodist) are primary sources. Union Parish Library has local history materials. D'Arbonne area families. Named for the political union of the states (before secession). Cross-reference with Ouachita, Lincoln, and Claiborne for related families. Timber industry heritage from late 1800s. BLM land patents for original entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1844; antebellum parish; Acadian/Cajun heartland
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Vermilion Parish Clerk of Court (Abbeville). Formed 1844 from Lafayette. Major Acadian/Cajun parish — Abbeville was founded by Father Antoine Desire Megret, a French priest. Vermilion Bay and Gulf coast. Catholic church registers at St. Mary Magdalen (Abbeville) and surrounding missions essential for Acadian families. Sugar and rice farming heritage. Pre-1844 records in Lafayette (Attakapas). Vermilion Parish Library has local history. Erath (Acadian Museum) documents Cajun heritage. Cross-reference with Lafayette and Iberia for related Acadian families. Cheniere (ridge) communities along the coast.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1871; Reconstruction-era formation; Central Louisiana
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Vernon Parish Clerk of Court (Leesville). Formed 1871 from Sabine, Natchitoches, and Rapides. Fort Polk (now Joint Readiness Training Center) is the major military installation — check NARA for military records. Pre-1871 records split among parent parishes. Western Louisiana piney woods. Anglo-American settlement predominates. Vernon Parish Library has local history. Leesville was a lumber town. Named for Mount Vernon (George Washington's estate). Protestant church records are primary sources. Cross-reference with Sabine, Rapides, and Beauregard for related families. Kisatchie National Forest area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1819; antebellum parish; Florida Parishes (former Spanish West Florida)
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Washington Parish Clerk of Court (Franklinton). Formed 1819 from St. Tammany. Florida Parishes region — part of Spanish West Florida and Republic of West Florida (1810). Anglo-American settlement predominates. Pre-1819 records in St. Tammany and Spanish colonial records at Louisiana State Archives. Franklinton area families. Pine forests and farming heritage. Protestant churches (Baptist, Methodist) predominate. Washington Parish Library has local history collections. Bogalusa (Great Southern Lumber Company) for early 1900s lumber boom research. Cross-reference with St. Tammany and St. Helena.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1871; Reconstruction-era formation; North Louisiana uplands
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Webster Parish Clerk of Court (Minden). Formed 1871 from Claiborne, Bossier, and Bienville. North Louisiana uplands. Pre-1871 records split among parent parishes. Minden area families. Cotton farming and later oil/gas economy. Webster Parish Library has local genealogical materials. Germantown Colony Museum (1835 utopian community) has records. Protestant church records predominate. Dorcheat Bayou area. Cross-reference with Claiborne and Bossier for earlier records. Named for Daniel Webster. Louisiana Military Museum in Ruston has some regional military records.
